title: Release Notes & Frequently Asked Questions | Respiratory Illnesses | CDC
text:
rveillance Network (RESP-NET) is now being published again. 
Footnotes about the exclusion of flu hospitalization data have been removed and updates have been added to the corresponding RESP-NET data notes. 
Why: 
FluSurv-NET surveillance for the 2024-2025 season ended on April 30, marking the beginning of the off-season. Surveillance for the 2025-2026 season began on October 1. 
August 8, 2025 release 
1. Launch of New Page - "Other Respiratory Illnesses Going Around"
What: 
Added a new page titled " Other Respiratory Illness Going Around ." 
This page is designed to inform the general public about various respiratory viruses and bacteria beyond COVID-19, flu and RSV. 
Included in the new page is a chart of non-pathogen-specific clinical condition trends by age group observed in emergency departments. 
Why: 
Our objective is to provide data for a wider range of respiratory illnesses. In doing so, we strive to elevate community knowledge and insight into the evolving landscape of respiratory illness. 
Providing data on clinical conditions helps users understand the impact of respiratory illnesses on different age demographics within emergency departments. 
2. Design updates for greater usability in February and early March 2025
Update #1 
What: A combo box has been implemented for county and state filtering on the Your Community Snapshot page. 
Why: User feedback indicated conf
